channel.title: The Adam and Dr. Drew Show
channel.description: Adam Carolla & Dr. Drew Pinsky reunite the partnership that made Loveline a wild success and cultural touchstone. In each episode Adam and Drew take uncensored, nothing-off-limits, calls about sex, drug, medical and relationship issues. Dr. Drew brings the medicine while Adam's comedy and rants are the spoonful of sugar to make it go down.
channel.itunes:author: PodcastOne / Carolla Digital
channel.itunes:summary: Adam Carolla & Dr. Drew Pinsky reunite the partnership that made Loveline a wild success and cultural touchstone. In each episode Adam and Drew take uncensored, nothing-off-limits, calls about sex, drug, medical and relationship issues. Dr. Drew brings the medicine while Adam's comedy and rants are the spoonful of sugar to make it go down.

image.title: The Adam and Dr. Drew Show

item.title: #2095 - What really happened to Lindsey Graham? | Part 2
item.description:
Adam and Dr. Drew react to the trailer for the 2003 film Grand Theft Parsons before diving into why critics' scores and audience ratings are often worlds apart. They also discuss young people's growing reliance on government, and Adam explains why comedy remains one of the few professions where talent matters more than age or appearance.

See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

They also discuss young people's growing reliance on government, and Adam explains why comedy remains one of the few professions where talent matters more than age or appearance.
See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.
item.content:encoded:
Adam and Dr. Drew react to the trailer for the 2003 film Grand Theft Parsons before diving into why critics' scores and audience ratings are often worlds apart. They also discuss young people's growing reliance on government, and Adam explains why comedy remains one of the few professions where talent matters more than age or appearance.

See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

item.title: Classic #1700 Jason Momoa Vs Chris Pratt
item.description:
March 30, 2023

Adam and Drew hear from a man going through the process of adopting in Germany and someone with thoughts on Jason Momoa. Next they take a dive into the speaking style of Willie Wilson.



See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

item.title: #2095 - What really happened to Lindsey Graham? | Part 1
item.description:
Adam and Dr. Drew kick things off by discussing the recent passing of Lindsay Graham, with Dr. Drew explaining what really caused his death and reacting to Dr. Oz's demonstration of the heart condition behind it. Adam also reflects on a classic John Denver lyric, and the two take a nostalgic trip through the world of Dr. Demento and the novelty songs that defined an era.

See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

Adam also reflects on a classic John Denver lyric, and the two take a nostalgic trip through the world of Dr. Demento and the novelty songs that defined an era.

Adam also reflects on a classic John Denver lyric, and the two take a nostalgic trip through the world of Dr. Demento and the novelty songs that defined an era.

See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

item.title: Classic #1681 “He Shit Me Up”
item.description:
February 14, 2023

Mark and Drew continue their week together and they open the show discussing the legal ramifications surrounding covid and the “misinformation” term that was wieldy so wildly during the pandemic. Later they pivot to a story that Mark has been covering closely on ‘Reasonable Doubt’ and ‘Beyond a Reasonable Doubt’ – the Alex Murdaugh murder case out of South Carolina.



See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

February 14, 2023

Mark and Drew continue their week together and they open the show discussing the legal ramifications surrounding covid and the “misinformation” term that was wieldy so wildly during the pandemic. Later they pivot to a story that Mark has been covering closely on ‘Reasonable Doubt’ and ‘Beyond a Reasonable Doubt’ – the Alex Murdaugh murder case out of South Carolina.



See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

item.title: #2094 - Another Democrat Sexual Assault Scandal + What’s Worse: Climate Change or Racism? | Part 2
item.description:
Adam and Dr. Drew debate what has been more detrimental to society: climate change or racism. They also discuss how the news can lead people to believe things that are not remotely true, before Adam introduces Dr. Drew to a new word. Later, Adam reflects on how the idea of being alone used to give him anxiety when he was younger, while now he genuinely enjoys his alone time. The guys also talk about how people used to experience boredom much more often than they do today, making it more important to find productive things to do, and Adam closes by recounting the long journey of apartment hopping he went through in the decade before starting Loveline with Dr. Drew.



See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

item.itunes:summary:
Adam and Dr. Drew debate what has been more detrimental to society: climate change or racism. They also discuss how the news can lead people to believe things that are not remotely true, before Adam introduces Dr. Drew to a new word. Later, Adam reflects on how the idea of being alone used to give him anxiety when he was younger, while now he genuinely enjoys his alone time. The guys also talk about how people used to experience boredom much more often than they do today, making it more important to find productive things to do, and Adam closes by recounting the long journey of apartment hopping he went through in the decade before starting Loveline with Dr. Drew.
See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

Adam and Dr. Drew debate what has been more detrimental to society: climate change or racism. They also discuss how the news can lead people to believe things that are not remotely true, before Adam introduces Dr. Drew to a new word. Later, Adam reflects on how the idea of being alone used to give him anxiety when he was younger, while now he genuinely enjoys his alone time. The guys also talk about how people used to experience boredom much more often than they do today, making it more important to find productive things to do, and Adam closes by recounting the long journey of apartment hopping he went through in the decade before starting Loveline with Dr. Drew.

item.title: Classic #1755 Beauty, Brains & Heart
item.description:
August 6, 2023

Adam and Dr. Drew welcomes someone from their Loveline past, the incomparable

Brandy Ledford who is also known from the popular show, ‘Baywatch Hawaii’. They

discuss her acting career highlights, as well as some personal battles and triumphs that

have led to her leading involvement in important nonprofits. Both Brandy and Dr. Drew

speaks very highly of the treatment center, ‘Friendly House’ and all the ways it’s helped

Brandy and countless others.



See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

speaks very highly of the treatment center, ‘Friendly House’ and all the ways it’s helped

item.title: #2094 - Another Democrat Sexual Assault Scandal + What’s Worse: Climate Change or Racism? | Part 1
item.description:
Adam and Dr. Drew open the episode by discussing the Graham Platner controversy and reacting to a clip of two reporters weighing in on the situation. Adam and Dr. Drew break down why they think the reporters are getting it wrong before shifting gears into one of Adam’s classic everyday frustrations: people who refuse to rinse their dirty dishes in the sink. Adam rants about the type of person who leaves food-crusted dishes behind and then explains to Dr. Drew a certain arrangement between men and women that has never quite made sense to him.



See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

Adam and Dr. Drew break down why they think the reporters are getting it wrong before shifting gears into one of Adam’s classic everyday frustrations: people who refuse to rinse their dirty dishes in the sink. Adam rants about the type of person who leaves food-crusted dishes behind and then explains to Dr. Drew a certain arrangement between men and women that has never quite made sense to him.
See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

Adam and Dr. Drew open the episode by discussing the Graham Platner controversy and reacting to a clip of two reporters weighing in on the situation. Adam and Dr. Drew break down why they think the reporters are getting it wrong before shifting gears into one of Adam’s classic everyday frustrations: people who refuse to rinse their dirty dishes in the sink. Adam rants about the type of person who leaves food-crusted dishes behind and then explains to Dr. Drew a certain arrangement between men and women that has never quite made sense to him.



See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

item.title: Classic #1744 Adam Is ”Newsweek” Infamous
item.description:
July 11, 2023

Adam and Dr. Drew dived right into a “Newsweek” article from the gates of hell that were 2020. Line by line he eviscerates the author’s erroneous points. Adam and Dr. Drew further illustrates their perspectives by using various examples, emphasizing all the egregious ways so many people behaved towards one another during this time. People claimed health concerns but ultimately, it was predominantly politically fueled jargon.



See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

Adam and Dr. Drew further illustrates their perspectives by using various examples, emphasizing all the egregious ways so many people behaved towards one another during this time. People claimed health concerns but ultimately, it was predominantly politically fueled jargon.

item.title: #2093 - How Blue Cities Became HELL ON EARTH! | Part 2
item.description:
Adam and Dr. Drew react to a clip about sugary sodas before getting into a larger conversation about SNAP, food stamps, welfare, personal responsibility, and what government assistance should or should not cover. They discuss the idea of limiting aid to basic, healthier foods, the lack of shared common ground in modern politics, and then shift into the hilarious “Chuck the Gruntled” bit with riffs on pre-gruntled, post-gruntled, and transgruntled. Later, Adam shares observations from walking along PCH, including fast-food stops, black marks on the pavement, litter, gum, and the habits that separate one area from another.


See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

They discuss the idea of limiting aid to basic, healthier foods, the lack of shared common ground in modern politics, and then shift into the hilarious “Chuck the Gruntled” bit with riffs on pre-gruntled, post-gruntled, and transgruntled. Later, Adam shares observations from walking along PCH, including fast-food stops, black marks on the pavement, litter, gum, and the habits that separate one area from another.
See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

Adam and Dr. Drew react to a clip about sugary sodas before getting into a larger conversation about SNAP, food stamps, welfare, personal responsibility, and what government assistance should or should not cover. They discuss the idea of limiting aid to basic, healthier foods, the lack of shared common ground in modern politics, and then shift into the hilarious “Chuck the Gruntled” bit with riffs on pre-gruntled, post-gruntled, and transgruntled. Later, Adam shares observations from walking along PCH, including fast-food stops, black marks on the pavement, litter, gum, and the habits that separate one area from another.


See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

item.title: Classic #1724 Gavin Newsom’s Night Out
item.description:
May 25, 2023

Adam and Drew take a deep dive on Gaivin Newsom’s outdoor dining regulations during the pandemic and compare it to his own public dining experiences at that time. Next, they remember the executive at SoFi stadium’s regulations during Super Bowl LVI and Drew wonders why he still has a job.



See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

item.title: #2093 - How Blue Cities Became HELL ON EARTH! | Part 1
item.description:
Adam and Dr. Drew discuss the movement of people and businesses out of high-tax cities like New York and into lower-tax, more business-friendly states like Florida. They break down how taxes, property ownership, sanctuary city policies, and local incentives shape where people choose to live and work, while also looking at how Hollywood and the entertainment industry are leaving less production-friendly areas for better opportunities elsewhere. The conversation expands into a broader discussion about politics, work ethic, entitlement, productivity, and why people respond to incentives the way they do.



See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

The conversation expands into a broader discussion about politics, work ethic, entitlement, productivity, and why people respond to incentives the way they do.
See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

Adam and Dr. Drew discuss the movement of people and businesses out of high-tax cities like New York and into lower-tax, more business-friendly states like Florida. They break down how taxes, property ownership, sanctuary city policies, and local incentives shape where people choose to live and work, while also looking at how Hollywood and the entertainment industry are leaving less production-friendly areas for better opportunities elsewhere. The conversation expands into a broader discussion about politics, work ethic, entitlement, productivity, and why people respond to incentives the way they do.

item.title: Classic #1721 The Ultimate F*** You
item.description:
May 18, 2023

Adam and Drew take a deep dive into the reason CDC Director Rochelle Walensky resigned. Next, Adam revisits his greatest moment of all time where he delivered the ultimate “FU”.

See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

item.title: #2092 - Empathy is a SUPERPOWER! | Part 2
item.description:
Adam and Dr. Drew reflect on how dramatically high school and society have changed since they were young, with Adam recalling why things like yearbooks and school photos were never priorities in his household. The conversation turns to the KROQ documentary screening they attended over the weekend and how it highlighted the cultural differences between the 1980s, 1990s, and today. They also discuss changing gender roles, major world events that permanently altered their perspectives, and react to actor John C. Reilly's comments about empathy being a superpower.



See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

item.title: #2092 - Empathy is a SUPERPOWER! | Part 1
item.description:
On this episode of The Adam & Dr. Drew Show, Dr. Daniel Amen joins the show to discuss the science of brain health and the surprising insights he's gained from scanning the brains of celebrities, substance abusers, and even serial killers. Dr. Amen explains how alcohol can contribute to brain diseases such as Alzheimer's, breaks down the risks associated with the growing popularity of nicotine pouches, and discusses why obesity can be so damaging to overall health. He also shares recommendations for supplements and lifestyle changes that may help improve brain function and reduce the risk of cognitive decline.

See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

Dr. Amen explains how alcohol can contribute to brain diseases such as Alzheimer's, breaks down the risks associated with the growing popularity of nicotine pouches, and discusses why obesity can be so damaging to overall health. He also shares recommendations for supplements and lifestyle changes that may help improve brain function and reduce the risk of cognitive decline.

item.content:encoded:
On this episode of The Adam & Dr. Drew Show, Dr. Daniel Amen joins the show to discuss the science of brain health and the surprising insights he's gained from scanning the brains of celebrities, substance abusers, and even serial killers. Dr. Amen explains how alcohol can contribute to brain diseases such as Alzheimer's, breaks down the risks associated with the growing popularity of nicotine pouches, and discusses why obesity can be so damaging to overall health. He also shares recommendations for supplements and lifestyle changes that may help improve brain function and reduce the risk of cognitive decline.

See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.
